result: If I could change the world in just one way, I would make empathy the default mode of human interaction—shifting the collective mindset so that everyone naturally seeks to understand and care for one another. In that reality, decisions would be weighed not just by immediate gain but by the impact on others, and systems (economics, politics, education) would be built around shared well‑being rather than competition and exclusion. A world grounded in deep, automatic compassion would likely curb many of the conflicts, inequities, and environmental crises we face today, because people would instinctively prioritize the health of the planet and the dignity of every person. It’s an ambitious vision, but fostering that fundamental shift in how we relate to each other could ripple outward into every other improvement we hope to achieve.
